# @delhivery/react-dashboard-boilerplate

A comprehensive React authentication library for React 18+ applications with OIDC (OpenID Connect) support, built-in header component, user profile management, and protected routes.

## Features

- 🔐 **OIDC Authentication** - Full OpenID Connect support using `oidc-client-ts`
- 🎯 **TypeScript Support** - Fully typed with TypeScript
- 📦 **Tree-shakeable** - ES modules and CommonJS support
- 🔄 **React 18+ Compatible** - Built for modern React
- 🛡️ **Protected Routes** - Easy route protection component
- 🎨 **Configurable Header** - Built-in header component with customization
- 👤 **User Profile** - Complete user profile management component
- 🔗 **Axios Interceptor** - Automatic token injection for API calls
- 🔑 **Permission Service** - Built-in permission checking utilities
- 🎣 **Easy-to-use Hooks** - Simple React hooks API

## Installation

### Install from GitHub Package Registry

First, configure npm to use GitHub Package Registry for the `@delhivery` scope. Create or update your `.npmrc` file:

```bash
@delhivery:registry=https://npm.pkg.github.com
//npm.pkg.github.com/:_authToken=YOUR_GITHUB_TOKEN
```

Then install the package:

```bash
npm install @delhivery/react-dashboard-boilerplate
# or
yarn add @delhivery/react-dashboard-boilerplate
# or
pnpm add @delhivery/react-dashboard-boilerplate
```

### GitHub Token Setup

To install packages from GitHub Package Registry, you need a GitHub Personal Access Token (PAT) with `read:packages` permission:

1. Go to GitHub Settings → Developer settings → Personal access tokens → Tokens (classic)
2. Generate a new token with `read:packages` scope
3. Add it to your `.npmrc` file or use it as an environment variable

## Peer Dependencies

This library requires the following peer dependencies:

```json
{
  "react": "^18.0.0",
  "react-dom": "^18.0.0",
  "react-router-dom": "^6.20.0",
  "axios": "^1.6.0",
  "oidc-client-ts": "^2.1.0"
}
```

Install them in your project:

```bash
npm install react react-dom react-router-dom axios oidc-client-ts
```

## Quick Start

### 1. Configure and Wrap your App with AuthProvider

```tsx
import { AuthProvider } from '@delhivery/react-dashboard-boilerplate';
import type { AuthConfig } from '@delhivery/react-dashboard-boilerplate';

const authConfig: AuthConfig = {
  clientId: 'your-client-id',
  default_issuer: 'https://your-issuer.com/realms/your-realm',
  landing_page: '/dashboard'
};

function App() {
  return (
    <AuthProvider config={authConfig}>
      <YourApp />
    </AuthProvider>
  );
}
```

### 2. Use the useAuth Hook

```tsx
import { useAuth } from '@delhivery/react-dashboard-boilerplate';

function LoginButton() {
  const { login, logout, user, isAuthenticated, isLoading } = useAuth();

  if (isLoading) {
    return <div>Loading...</div>;
  }

  if (isAuthenticated) {
    return (
      <div>
        <p>Welcome, {user?.profile?.name}!</p>
        <button onClick={logout}>Logout</button>
      </div>
    );
  }

  return (
    <button
      onClick={() => {
        const issuer = 'https://your-issuer.com/realms/your-realm';
        const clientId = 'your-client-id';
        const redirectUri = `${window.location.origin}/lib-auth-oauth-callback`;
        login(issuer, clientId, redirectUri);
      }}
    >
      Login
    </button>
  );
}
```

### 3. Protect Routes

```tsx
import { ProtectedRoute, AuthRoutes } from '@delhivery/react-dashboard-boilerplate';
import { Routes, Route } from 'react-router-dom';

function App() {
  return (
    <Routes>
      {/* Include auth routes */}
      {...AuthRoutes}
      
      {/* Protected route */}
      <Route
        path="/dashboard"
        element={
          <ProtectedRoute fallback={<div>Loading...</div>}>
            <Dashboard />
          </ProtectedRoute>
        }
      />
      
      {/* Public route */}
      <Route path="/login" element={<Login />} />
    </Routes>
  );
}
```

### 4. Configure Header

```tsx
import { useHeaderConfig } from '@delhivery/react-dashboard-boilerplate';

function App() {
  const { setHeaderConfig } = useHeaderConfig();

  useEffect(() => {
    setHeaderConfig({
      title: 'My Application',
      logoSrc: '/assets/logo.png',
      logoAlt: 'App Logo',
      navItems: [
        { label: 'Home', route: '/home' },
        { label: 'Dashboard', route: '/dashboard' },
        { label: 'Settings', route: '/settings' }
      ],
      otherComponent: <CustomHeaderComponent />
    });
  }, [setHeaderConfig]);

  return <YourAppContent />;
}
```

## Complete Example

```tsx
import { 
  AuthProvider, 
  useAuth, 
  ProtectedRoute, 
  AuthRoutes,
  useHeaderConfig 
} from '@delhivery/react-dashboard-boilerplate';
import { Routes, Route, Navigate } from 'react-router-dom';
import type { AuthConfig } from '@delhivery/react-dashboard-boilerplate';

const authConfig: AuthConfig = {
  clientId: 'my-client-id',
  default_issuer: 'https://keycloak.example.com/realms/my-realm',
  landing_page: '/dashboard'
};

function AppContent() {
  const { isAuthenticated, login, isLoading } = useAuth();
  const { setHeaderConfig } = useHeaderConfig();

  useEffect(() => {
    setHeaderConfig({
      title: 'My App',
      logoSrc: '/assets/logo.svg',
      navItems: [
        { label: 'Dashboard', route: '/dashboard' }
      ]
    });
  }, [setHeaderConfig]);

  useEffect(() => {
    if (!isAuthenticated && !isLoading) {
      const issuer = authConfig.default_issuer;
      const clientId = authConfig.clientId;
      const redirectUri = `${window.location.origin}/lib-auth-oauth-callback`;
      login(issuer, clientId, redirectUri);
    }
  }, [isAuthenticated, isLoading, login]);

  if (isLoading) {
    return <div>Loading...</div>;
  }

  return (
    <Routes>
      {...AuthRoutes}
      <Route
        path="/dashboard"
        element={
          <ProtectedRoute>
            <Dashboard />
          </ProtectedRoute>
        }
      />
      <Route path="/" element={<Navigate to="/dashboard" replace />} />
    </Routes>
  );
}

function App() {
  return (
    <AuthProvider config={authConfig}>
      <AppContent />
    </AuthProvider>
  );
}

export default App;
```

## API Reference

### AuthProvider

The main provider component that manages authentication state and OIDC configuration.

#### Props

```typescript
interface AuthProviderProps {
  config: AuthConfig;
  children: ReactNode;
}

interface AuthConfig {
  clientId: string;           // OIDC client ID
  default_issuer: string;      // OIDC issuer URL (e.g., https://keycloak.com/realms/my-realm)
  landing_page: string;        // Landing page route after authentication
}
```

#### Example

```tsx
<AuthProvider
  config={{
    clientId: 'my-client-id',
    default_issuer: 'https://keycloak.example.com/realms/my-realm',
    landing_page: '/dashboard'
  }}
>
  <App />
</AuthProvider>
```

### useAuth Hook

Hook to access authentication state and methods.

#### Returns

```typescript
{
  user: User | null;                    // Current OIDC user object
  isAuthenticated: boolean;             // Whether user is authenticated
  isLoading: boolean;                   // Whether auth state is loading
  login: (issuer: string, clientId: string, redirectUri: string) => Promise<void>;
  logout: () => Promise<void>;
  getUserManager: () => UserManager | null;  // Get OIDC UserManager instance
}
```

#### Example

```tsx
function MyComponent() {
  const { user, isAuthenticated, login, logout } = useAuth();

  const handleLogin = async () => {
    await login(
      'https://keycloak.example.com/realms/my-realm',
      'my-client-id',
      `${window.location.origin}/lib-auth-oauth-callback`
    );
  };

  return (
    <div>
      {isAuthenticated ? (
        <div>
          <p>Welcome, {user?.profile?.name}</p>
          <button onClick={logout}>Logout</button>
        </div>
      ) : (
        <button onClick={handleLogin}>Login</button>
      )}
    </div>
  );
}
```

### ProtectedRoute

Component that only renders children when user is authenticated.

#### Props

```typescript
interface ProtectedRouteProps {
  children: ReactNode;
  fallback?: ReactNode;  // Component to show while loading (default: 'Loading...')
}
```

#### Example

```tsx
<ProtectedRoute fallback={<Spinner />}>
  <Dashboard />
</ProtectedRoute>
```

### useHeaderConfig Hook

Hook to configure the header component.

#### Returns

```typescript
{
  headerConfig: HeaderConfig;
  setHeaderConfig: (config: HeaderConfig) => void;
}

interface HeaderConfig {
  title?: string | ReactNode;
  logoSrc?: string;
  logoAlt?: string;
  navItems?: Array<{ label: string; route: string }>;
  otherComponent?: ReactNode;
}
```

#### Example

```tsx
function App() {
  const { setHeaderConfig } = useHeaderConfig();

  useEffect(() => {
    setHeaderConfig({
      title: 'My Application',
      logoSrc: '/assets/logo.png',
      navItems: [
        { label: 'Home', route: '/home' },
        { label: 'Dashboard', route: '/dashboard' }
      ]
    });
  }, [setHeaderConfig]);

  return <YourApp />;
}
```

### AuthRoutes

Array of Route elements for auth-related routes. Spread these into your Routes component.

#### Routes Included

- `/lib-auth-oauth-callback` - OAuth callback handler
- `/lib-user-profile` - User profile page

#### Example

```tsx
import { AuthRoutes } from '@delhivery/react-dashboard-boilerplate';
import { Routes, Route } from 'react-router-dom';

<Routes>
  {...AuthRoutes}
  <Route path="/dashboard" element={<Dashboard />} />
</Routes>
```

### Components

#### Header

Built-in header component that displays based on HeaderConfig. Automatically included when using AuthProvider.

#### UserProfile

Complete user profile management component. Accessible at `/lib-user-profile` route.

#### OAuthCallback

OAuth callback handler component. Automatically handles OAuth redirects.

### Services

#### loginService

Service for managing login/logout operations.

```typescript
import { loginService } from '@delhivery/react-dashboard-boilerplate';

// Get user manager
const userManager = loginService.getUserManager();

// Login
await loginService.login(issuer, clientId, redirectUri);

// Logout
await loginService.logout();
```

#### permissionService

Service for checking user permissions.

```typescript
import { permissionService } from '@delhivery/react-dashboard-boilerplate';

// Check permission
const hasPermission = permissionService.hasPermission(permission);
```

#### configService

Service for managing authentication configuration.

```typescript
import { configService } from '@delhivery/react-dashboard-boilerplate';

// Get config
const config = configService.getConfig();

// Set config
configService.setConfig(authConfig);
```

#### setupAuthInterceptor

Sets up axios interceptor for automatic token injection.

```typescript
import { setupAuthInterceptor } from '@delhivery/react-dashboard-boilerplate';

// Setup interceptor (usually called automatically by AuthProvider)
setupAuthInterceptor();
```

## Types

### User

The user object is from `oidc-client-ts` and includes:

```typescript
interface User {
  id_token: string;
  access_token: string;
  refresh_token?: string;
  token_type: string;
  scope: string;
  profile: {
    sub: string;
    name?: string;
    email?: string;
    [key: string]: any;
  };
  expires_at?: number;
  state?: any;
}
```

## Development

### Building the Library

```bash
# Install dependencies
npm install

# Build the library
npm run build:lib

# Type check
npm run type-check

# Development mode
npm run dev
```

### Project Structure

```
react-dashboard-boilerplate/
├── src/
│   └── auth/
│       ├── components/      # React components (Header, UserProfile, etc.)
│       ├── context/         # React contexts (AuthContext, HeaderConfigContext)
│       ├── services/        # Services (login, permission, config, interceptor)
│       ├── types/           # TypeScript types
│       ├── routes.tsx       # Auth routes
│       └── index.ts         # Main entry point
├── dist/                    # Built files (generated)
├── package.json
├── tsconfig.json
├── tsconfig.lib.json
└── vite.config.ts
```

## Publishing

The package is automatically published to GitHub Package Registry when a release is created. The workflow is configured in `.github/workflows/publish.yml`.
